title: Idle Hands
description:
Idle hands do the Devil's work. Tap to summon demons, raise an infernal workforce, and claw your way down through the nine Circles of Hell... from Limbo to the frozen treachery at the bottom, where the Devil himself is waiting.

Descend, and grow strongerEvery descent is a bargain: you give up everything you've built -- your demons, your power-ups, your whole workforce -- for a permanent multiplier that makes the next circle pay out far more. Nine circles, each richer and stranger than the last.

 

Bargain at the CrossroadsA figure waits where the roads cross, and he deals in souls. Stake what you've earned on a single roll: bet small and the odds turn against you; bet big and the swings turn brutal. Walk away with a fortune... or get Cast Down to Limbo for your greed. The dial is yours. So is the risk.

Battle the DevilReach the bottom and Satan takes notice. He'll steal from your hoard and dare you to take it back -- a tapping duel that escalates every time you win. Beat him, and he comes back stronger. The fight never ends the same way twice.

Bank souls, climb foreverResurrect to convert a run into Souls, then spend them on permanent powers that carry across every future descent. It's an idle game that respects your time -- play actively and tap, or set it down and let your demons earn while you're away.

Features• Nine Circles of Hell, each a soft-prestige with its own multiplier

• Two upgrade tracks: power-ups (per tap) and recruiters (idle income)

• The Crossroads: a soul-staking gamble with a real risk/reward dial

• The Devil:  an escalating tap-battle boss you can keep challenging

• Resurrection meta-prestige with permanent soul upgrades

• 19 achievements, from your first demon to a million-clicks

• Plays in short bursts or long idle sessions
description_short: Summon demons, descend the nine Circles of Hell, and gamble your soul with the Devil. A wicked idle clicker... multiply your power with every descent, tempt fate at the Crossroads, and battle Satan in a duel that never plays out the same way twice.
